problem.title: Print Linked List in Reversed Order
problem.description: Print a linked list in reversed order using recursion or iterative approach.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Recursive
solutions.description: Recurse to the end of the list before printing: each call first processes the rest of the list, then prints the current node's value on the way back up. This naturally prints nodes in reverse without any explicit reversal or extra data structure.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next

def printLinkedListInReverse(head: Optional[object]) -> None:
    if not head:
        return
    printLinkedListInReverse(head.next)
    print(head.val)
